entry.title: Deletion log
entry.content:
Good morning, everyone, how are you?

I’m building a data mesh with my SAP data, and I ran into an issue.

As a strategy, I used Kafka to collect my inserts and updates in real time. After that, the data is sent to BigQuery.

I had a case where a user created a purchase order with 5 lines, but the next day she deleted 4 of them.

Is there any type of record that shows me the deleted lines?

How does SAP B1 build the SELECT statement for the modification logs? Because in the system, I can see when a line was removed.

Thank you for your help.
Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Recap Series: SAP Community members speaking at SAP Sapphire 2025
entry.content:
Nice to meetyou! I'm an SAP FICO Consultant from Brazil (Recife/PE) and an active SAP Community member sice 2007. I enjoy supporting and helping organize SAP Inside Track events across the country. After the sessions, I usually use ChatGPT's YouTube Video Summarizer to quickly create recap blog posts. I combine short summaries, video replays, and key links to make the content easily accessible for the community. I use this prompt:

write a blog post about the session in the below link, presented at SAP Inside Track Recife 2024. The blog should include the following elements: Title of the presentation, A teaser or introduction to the session, Name and bio of the speaker, Description of the session, Main blog content with a summary of the presentation, Do not use emojis, images, or graphic elements.

URL
https://www.youtube.com/live/nFbbEjbNwbI

It's a way to keep the knowledge flowing right after the event and encourage more people to engage with what was shared.
Does anyone have a faster way? With this one, it takes me around 4' to 5' per blog.

entry.title: Re: Help Global System Settings FIN-CS
entry.content:
Hi MUrilo Machado,

Could you please confirm whether you are using ECCS or Group Reporting (GR) in your older S/4HANA version?

If you are using ECCS within S/4HANA, you will need to follow the migration scenario before proceeding.

If you are already using Group Reporting, you can activate the relevant fields via SPRO.( S4HANA Group Reporting-Global setting for consolidation.

Important: If you are still on ECCS, please do not activate any Group Reporting content until you have consulted with SAP Support or the SAP Consulting/Services team to avoid potential conflicts in the ECCS and GR tables/ledgers etc.

entry.title: How can I automatically send the PO as a PDF to the creator of the PO via Email in S/4 Public Cloud?
entry.content:
Hello dear SAP community,

in this helpful article, there are instructions on how to set up the output configuration in a way that the PO is sent to the supplier via E-Mail after creating it:

Know-how to set-up documented emails sent automati... - SAP Community

But how can I set up the following scenario?

Whenever a PO is created, the system should automatically send the PO PDF to the creator of the PO (user who created it) or the Purchasing Group via E-Mail, the user will then forward the PO to the supplier manually. The supplier should not receive the PO directly from the system.

I am aware that the standard output in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud is typically sent directly to the supplier based on the output parameter determination rules.

Is there a standard way (or a best practice) to automatically send the PO PDF via email to the user who created the PO or at least to the E-Mail behind a Purchasing Group? 

I’ve explored the “Output Parameter Determination” app, but I haven’t found a way to dynamically set the email recipient based on the PO creator.

Any ideas, suggestions, or experiences are highly appreciated. 

Thank you very much in advance for your support!

Best regards,

Api

entry.title: Production Planning Optimization (PPO) - Part IV
entry.content:
Introduction

In PP/DS we have been introduced with a Production Planning Optimization (PPO) functionality for a while. In this blog series we are investigating this tool.

This blog is the last part of my PPO blog series, successor of "Production Planning Optimization (PPO) - Part III" (Production Planning Optimization (PPO) - Part III - SAP Community).

In this part, we are investigating how to execute PPO, what to consider before execution, where to check results, how to evaluate the logs.

 

Ways of PPO Execution

Independent Execution

PPO execution has its own transaction to run: /N/SAPAPO/PPO_OPT - Production Planning Optimizer Run.

Execution as a Step in Production Planning Run

There is a specific heuristic that can be copied as a template to execute PPO within production planning run: SAP_PP_PPO - PP Optimizer.

To make PPO part of production planning run as a step; copy SAP_PP_PPO heuristic, change parameters accordingly, use the new heuristic within one of the steps of production planning run.

Note: In below sections, PPO will be executed via /N/SAPAPO/PPO_OPT transaction.

 

PPO Selection Screen

First remember the selection parameters:

We have:

  • Planning Version
  • Propagation Range
  • Start and End Dates
  • Location-Products Selection
  • PPO Profiles

Note: You can ignore "Settings for DS Optimizer", but keep in mind that you can automatically trigger DS Optimizer right after PP Optimizer; which I am not planning to use or tell. For further investigation please check: Integrating DS Optimizer and PP Optimizer | SAP Help Portal

Planning Version

Set as 000.

Propagation Range

Set as SAPALL.

Planning Horizon

As we know, MRP considers entire time horizon starting from big-bang up to PP/DS horizon. PP/DS horizon is set within the planning version via /N/SAPAPO/MVM transaction or defined separately for each material on material master Advanced Planning tab.

On the other hand; PP Optimizer works within a time window defined.

Then; PPO will consider all requirements before "Planning Start Date" as customer orders on first bucket, and PPO will consider all receipts before "Planning Start Date" as initial stock on first bucket.

Moreover, PPO will not consider any requirement or receipt after "Planning End Date". In other words, PPO is blind beyond the end date.

Note: Do not maintain Planning Start and End dates in the past, since PPO does not perform in the past. But don't worry, since it will consider backlogs anyway, as mentioned above.

Note: Although technically possible, avoid having half periods. If you have daily buckets, start PPO from at least tomorrow; if weekly buckets, start PPO from at least next Monday; if monthly buckets, start PPO from next 1st day of next month. Accordingly, planning end date of PPO should be the last day of related bucket.

Note: PPO can work in sub-daily buckets (shifts) as well; however, I doubt to use it, because customer orders or planned independent requirements are all timed to 00:00:00, which does not reflect exact sub-daily bucket. Therefore; I prefer using daily bucket minimum.

How Should Planning Horizon Be Determined?

In long term; MRP is executed to ensure every material is procured or produced regardless from any constraint, so that long-lead raw materials can be delivered anyway and capacity/production reports can be utilized.

In mid-term; PPO is executed to ensure feasible (constrained) production plan for the preparation of detailed scheduling and stable short-lead time raw materials procurement.

In short-term; detailed scheduling is carried out, production plans are fixed, and finally converted into manufacturing orders. We can call short-term as fixed schedule horizon as well. If there is no fixed schedule, still it is assumed to be 1 day (current day), since the production planner should already have created today's manufacturing orders in advance. Short-term is mainly processed by Gantt Chart (transaction /N/SAPAPO/CDPS0).

The definition of short-term and mid-term varies depending on industry, company process, and design. But in any case, PPO should cover mid-term horizon; which means it should start just 1 day after short-term.

Let's discuss over an example. As a production planner, assume we are working in weekly buckets. Current week was already scheduled and converted into manufacturing orders, last week. This week, we will schedule and fix next week. Except long-lead time raw materials, we have raw materials with planned delivery time up to 3 weeks.

In this case; short-term is week 0 (current week), mid-term is between week 1 (next week) and week 3 (3 weeks later), then comes long-term that starts from week 4 (4 weeks later). Therefore; PPO start date should be next Monday, and end date should be Sunday of 3 weeks later.

How to Segregate PPO and MRP

In order to avoid MRP affect PPO horizon; I suggest defining "Fixing Interval" for each related resource via SAP_DS_03 heuristic, that creates fixing interval for related resources up to end of PPO horizon, so MRP cannot create planned orders interfering PPO horizon.

Before next PPO run, you can delete existing fixing intervals again via SAP_DS_03 heuristic.

Selection of Products

Here, the location-products that will subject to PPO is determined. Unincluded location-products will be ignored, so their stock availability or other constraints will not be taken into account.

Settings for Optimizer

Here, the profiles defined earlier in customizing is given.

Note: Be aware that PPO only considers ATP categories that are defined in Category Profile. If there is any requirement or receipt, whose ATP category is not defined in the Category Profile, it will be ignored by PPO.

 

PPO Execution

On selection screen of transaction /N/SAPAPO/PPO_OPT, press F8 and execute. Then you will see a log, that lists messages and shows that optimization run ended.

Now you can check Product View (transaction /N/SAPAPO/RRP3) or Receipts View (transaction /N/SAPAPO/RRP4) and see the planned orders created within PPO planning horizon. Based on our customizing setting of PPO Optimization Profile, within PPO horizon, unfirmed planned orders are deleted and replaced with newly created planned orders when needed.

Note: Planned Orders which have setup group/key defined on any operation are created as deallocated.

 

Interactive Planning After PPO

Since PPO is a periodical planning tool, best way to evaluate the results and work on them is to use Product Planning Table (transaction /N/SAPAPO/PPT1).

User Settings of Product Planning Table

Before using Product Planning Table, I suggest maintaining user settings as below.

On selection screen of Product Planning Table (transaction /N/SAPAPO/PPT1), choose menu Settings->User Settings.

Define user settings as seen on screenshots and save:

Mark "Active" checkbox for Receipts as well, on Product 1 tab.

Save the settings.

Using Product Planning Table

Call transaction /N/SAPAPO/PPT1 (Product Planning Table).

Maintain selection screen as below:

  • Planning Version = 000
  • From = Today
  • To = 1 year later
  • Location = The responsible plant
  • Product Planner = One or more product planners (MRP Controllers) we are dealing with

Execute and you will see a screen with 2 panes:

  • Upper Pane - Resource View Periodic
  • Lower Pane - Product View Periodic

On the upper pane, you will see the list of resources related to products selected, and each resources capacity utilization for each period.

Select the resource you want to work on, to see relevant products and their production plans loading the resource; then press the "Show Products for Resource" button to fill lower pane.

In the lower pane (Product View Periodic), you can see a periodical representation of each relevant product's requirements, receipts, and available quantities. You can change the quantities in each period below and see the effect on resource utilization above. You can revise production plans like working on an excel sheet.

Note: When double clicked on a cell with quantity, detailed order list will show up, both on Resource View Periodic and Product View Periodic.

Note: Notice that both panes have periods as defined in PPO Time Profile.

If changes are made, save (CTRL+S) the results.

Period of Elements at Midnight

Receipts ending sharp at midnight (00:00:00) or similarly requirements at midnight, all are seen on the former period.

This logic makes sense for receipts, but requirements should belong to latter period.

In order to satisfy this behavior in Product Planning Table, I suggest defining shift/days 1 minute shorter (e.g. 23:59:00 instead of 24:00:00 or 15:59:00 instead of 16:00:00). In order to compensate missing 1 minute, you can shorten on of the breaks 1 minute as well; thus, you will not lose any capacity.

However, this definition is not enough; in addition, you must implement BAdI /SAPAPO/PPT_FLEX_PER, and set EV_ADJUST_SHIFT parameter as 'X' within BUILD_PERIODS method.

 

Checking PPO Logs for Analysis

Sometimes it may be required to check PPO logs to understand the effects of master data changes, or consistency of master data, or PPO scope, etc.

In such times, a certain PPO Logs transaction can be checked via transaction /N/SAPAPO/PPO_LOG - Production Planning Optimizer Log.

Call the transaction and see the list of PPO runs logged.

Double click on the PPO run to check the logs.

On the left pane you can see the log files. You can double click any item of the list, and you can see the detailed list on the right pane.

The primary log files you need to know are:

  • Input Parameters: Selection screen/parameters of the PPO run.
  • Location Products: Product and Location combination list included in the PPO run.
  • Input Log
    • ET_BUCKDF: List of buckets, each with a counter code, as well as start and end times.
    • ET_LOCMAT: Priority, Storage Cost, Safety Penalty, Procurement Cost of each location product.
    • ET_RESOURCE: List of production resources included in the PPO run.
    • ET_ARC: Means of transports list included in the PPO run (Transportation Lane generic).
    • ET_ARCMAT: Means of transports list included in the PPO run (Product specific).
    • ET_RESC: Available capacities of production resources.
    • ET_PROMO: List of PDSs considered by the PPO run.
    • ET_PRORES: Resource capacity consumptions of each PDS.
    • ET_PROMAT: Input and output products and relevant quantities of each PDS.
    • ET_LOCPROD: Safety stock levels, fixed demands, and fixed receipts of each location product.
    • ET_DEMCLTIM: Non-delivery penalty, delay penalty, and maximum delay of each location product, separated by demand class (customer demand or forecast).
    • ET_DEMAND: List of customer demands and forecasts for each location product, bucket by bucket.
  • Results Log
    • IT_RESOURCE: Capacity consumption and available capacity of each production resource, bucket by bucket.
    • IT_ARCMAT: Stock transfer requisitions created.
    • IT_PROMO: Planned orders created.
    • IT_PROCUR: Purchase requisitions created.
    • IT_DEMAND: Fulfilled customer demands and forecasts.
    • IT_NOTDELI: Unfulfilled customer demands and forecasts.

Typical Lot Size

In PDS related log files (ET_PRORES and ET_PROMAT) includes capacity consumptions and material quantities based on a quantity called "Typical Lot Size".

Typical Lot Size is calculated for each PDS following below queries:

  1. If a fixed lot size is maintained in the product master, the typical lot size is equal to the fixed lot size.
  2. If a minimum lot size is maintained either in the product master or in the source of supply, the maximum of both minimum lot sizes is the typical lot size.
  3. If a rounding value is maintained in the product master, the rounding value is the typical lot size.
  4. If none of the above is applicable, lot size quantity of 1 is the typical lot size (fallback).

Typical Lot Size of a PDS can be seen in ET_PROMAT log file, as quantity of header material.

 

Epilogue

Having all these written and read, hereby, we conclude our PPO series.

I hope you enjoy and benefit from this series.

It was a pleasure for me to share all this content of 4 consecutive blogs covering such a great topic; which have taken almost 1 month to organize entire experience and information, including simultaneous testing.

I cannot wait to meet you with other blogs. Take care.

 

Previous Blog: Production Planning Optimization (PPO) - Part III - SAP Community

entry.title: How Smart Maintenance Is Changing the Game – A Journey Through Innovation with SAP
entry.content:
Telling a story of a case that happens repeatedly and often makes us wonder, ‘what was missing this time?’. It was a huge operation in Germany—rows of heavy machinery humming in perfect rhythm. Yet every few weeks, production would grind to a halt. A pump would fail. A belt would snap. A sensor would go silent.

The irony? They had the data. Sensors were everywhere. But the systems weren’t connected. The insights weren’t clear. The maintenance strategy was reactive.

You already know SAP Intelligent Asset Management ?

Came hear some of these examples at SAP Inside Track Porto 2025.

From Chaos to Control: The Power of Prediction

Their journey started with a simple question:
What if we could predict failures before they happen?

With SAP Predictive Asset Insights, they began feeding real-time sensor data into machine learning models. Over time, the system learned to recognize early warning signs, detect anomalies, and trigger alerts before a failure occurred.

Next, they implemented SAP Asset Strategy and Performance Management, defining smarter, risk-based maintenance strategies. What once was guesswork became a structured, data-informed plan.

And suddenly, downtime dropped. Efficiency climbed. The maintenance team stopped reacting and started anticipating.

A Network of Intelligence

But the real breakthrough came when they connected to the SAP Asset Intelligence Network. Now, OEMs, service providers, and plant operators could instantly share digital twins, manuals, and updates.

Imagine a technician in the field, standing in front of a faulty machine. With a tablet and SAP Mobile Asset Management, they could access the exact service history, recommended fix, and even a video from the manufacturer — all in seconds.

This wasn’t just smart maintenance. This was collaborative intelligence.

Real Stories Across Europe

And this isn’t just one plant. Across Europe, companies are rethinking asset management:

  • In France, utility giant Veolia uses SAP to monitor their vast water infrastructure in real time, improving service reliability while saving millions in emergency repairs.
  • In Germany, Deutsche Bahn monitors thousands of rail components, improving punctuality and reducing disruptions — all thanks to predictive insights.
  • In the Netherlands, energy provider Eneco uses IoT-enabled maintenance to keep wind turbines spinning, increasing uptime and boosting renewable energy output.

These aren’t tech demos. They’re business revolutions.

A New Era of Asset Management

What’s most exciting? This is just the beginning.

With artificial intelligence, IoT, and SAP S/4HANA, asset management is evolving into something far more powerful - a strategic pillar of resilience, performance, and sustainability.

Companies that embrace this transformation are no longer just fixing machines.
They are unlocking the full potential of their operations.

And the plant in Germany? It’s no longer silent from unexpected failures.
It’s running smarter, longer, and better than ever.

Tiago Fernandes  emphasized the importance of industry-standard data and taxonomy by partnering with Reliability Dynamics:

“Better decision‑making requires better Asset Management information, and better information requires better data. SAP & Reliability Dynamics provide an integrated tool to standardize Extended ISO 14224 taxonomy … failure catalogs … equipment characteristics.”  This reinforces the need for clean, structured data to power effective IAM implementations.

You can participate in this and other topics and listen to insights at SAP Inside Track Porto, where Tiago Fernandes  will speak.

References Veolia Case

  • Integration of asset and finance systems, mobile field data, and procurement directly in EAM/SAP teamwork.net+2istart.co.nz+2anz.veolia.com+2community.sap.com
  • Use of Hubgrade Blockage Prediction with machine learning to optimize pipeline investment watertechnologies.com+2anz.veolia.com+2veolia.com+2
  • SVG digital tools for leak detection, crisis management, and real-time plant insights watertechnologies.com+2veolia.com+2watertechnologies.com+2

References Deutsche Bahn

  • Predictive maintenance pilot with Siemens on Velaro D trains valantic.cominspirient.com+1reddit.com+1community.sap.compress.siemens.com
  • SAP mobile and APM integration along railways en.wikipedia.org+14valantic.com+14valantic.com+14
  • Inspirient’s AI analytics across 5,400 stations inspirient.com+1community.sap.com+1

References Eneco

  • SAP Service & Asset Manager implementation for mobile maintenance in heat networks sap.com+12ideo-nl.com+12arxiv.org+12ideo-nl.comcommunity.sap.com+4sap.com+4sap.com+4arxiv.org+1arxiv.org+1eneco.nl+1pulse.microsoft.com+1
  • Remote assistance via TeamViewer Frontline and SSAM ideo-nl.com
  • Cloud-first integration with SAP Integration Suite (50–60% middleware TCO reduction) community.sap.com+2sap.com+2sap.com+2

entry.title: Re: SAP MRM: What is the retention period of the job logs...
entry.content:
Hello,

The Request Current Market Data job executed via Schedule Treasury Back Office Jobs follows the standard retention settings of the SAP job management framework. Here's how it works:

Job Log Retention

  • The default retention period for job logs in the SAP system is typically 30 days.
  • This applies to all jobs executed via the job scheduler, including Treasury jobs, unless customized.
  • The cleanup is usually handled by background programs such as RSBTCDEL.

Spool Retention

  • Spool requests are handled by the TemSe system and follow a separate retention policy.
  • The default spool retention is usually 8 days, unless modified in transaction SPAD or through jobs like RSPO1041 (Spool deletion).

Recommendation

To ensure compliance and clarity:

  1. Ask your BASIS team to review job log retention via RSBTCDEL settings.
  2. Confirm the spool retention policy in SPAD or related deletion jobs.
  3. Adjust the retention periods if your business or audit requirements demand longer storage.

entry.title: Re: ABSENCE QUOTA CARRY OVER
entry.content:
What is the Time Type that allows you to generate an Allowance for Quota 16?  For this exercise, I will assume that it is Q16A. 

Create an Information Time Type to carry the Quota's Balance (such as Q15B).

Create a PCR to read the Balance of Quota 15 on the Last Day of the Quota's Validation Period, using Operation OUTAQTYP to query Quota 15, Operation HRS=QCREST to query it's balance, and then Operation ADDDB Q15B, and call that PCR through Function P2006.

Create an other PCR to query "Yesterday's" value of Time Type Q16B on the First Day of the Quota's Validation Period, using Operation HRS=LQ16B followed by ADDDBQ16A.

entry.title: Re: Custom CBC Customizations
entry.content:
Hello @vladimir_culic 

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, your requirement to maintain and transport a custom mapping (external SAP company codes) is a common scenario, but with specific limitations

 Key Points

  • Custom Business Objects (CBOs)
    • Can be created and exposed via API.
    • Data is not transportable across systems, only structure moves.
    • This is standard behaviour in the Public Cloud.
  • Custom configuration tables:
  • Created via key-user extensibility (e.g. Custom Tables in Custom Fields and Logic).
  • Data is transportable via customised transports (CBC).
  • But they are not API-exposable by default.
  • Activate Business Event Logging and annotations like @ObjectModel.configuration don’t apply here, they are not supported for this use case in Public Cloud.

SAP Help Custom Business Objects

SAP Recommendation

  • For API access → use CBO, maintain data separately per tenant.
  • For transportable data → use Customising Table (UI only).
  • For advanced control, → manage mapping externally via SAP BTP (CAP service).

Let me know if you need further information

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Creating a new Crystal Report and using two Parameters to filter data, then.....
entry.content:
Crystal Report Version :  2010

I already have the SQL Select statement to get the data, now, in order to filter the data using two Dates as a Date range, I need to create the Starting Date and End Date, I started doing that.  Then, I tried to use those two parameters in the COMMAND file I created, but is not working, it means all the data is passing. thru.

Those two parameters,  I need to use as the End Date : the Current Date, I created as p_CurrentDate.

And for the Beginning Date, I need to use a calculated value as :  The first Parameter minus 30 days,  it means  :  f_CurrentDate - 30 Days.

These  should be taken by default, to be run by SAP BI and creating an excel file.

Then.

First Paremeter :  p_CurrentDate :  Taking the CurrentDate from the System

Second Parameter :  p_CurrentDate - 30 Days.Date,

But, the user, also, could run the Crystal report to get a different period of time, it means the CurrentDate would be different, because it becomes the end date, then, the Begin Date sould be equeally 30 days before.

So, the first parameter ( p_CurrentDate ), should be flexible to get the really Current Date or to use a different date to caluclate the second parameter, maybe using a formula.

So, for some reason, when I run the report, is not taking the two parameters to filter the data and show only the records in that Date range.

May I missing something in the parameters Settings?

I attached a file with the setup of that Parameter, the second one is very similar.

entry.title: [SAP Build Process Automation] Desktop Agent does not synchronize deployed projects and stuck logs
entry.content:
Dear SAP Build Process Automation Community:

I'm having a persistent and very frustrating issue with my SAP Build Process Automation desktop agent. It doesn't seem to sync deployed projects despite seemingly correct configuration and several troubleshooting attempts. I would greatly appreciate any help, suggestions, or diagnostics based on your experiences.SAP Build Process Automation 

 

The core problem (with the desktop agent!):

My Desktop Agent (v3.37.57 on Windows 11) constantly displays the message "It appears no projects have been received yet" in the "Projects" window. Most worryingly, its local logs ("History/Events") are completely blocked, showing the last activity as [Exact date of capture, e.g., June 9, 2025], even though it is [Current date, e.g., June 18, 2025].

This indicates that the agent is not processing or updating its status locally, nor is it downloading projects that have been deployed.

 

Project context and configuration (Everything looks fine in the cloud!):

I've set up a project to automate the mass update of fixed asset master records in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. The cloud configuration appears to be correct:

Project: Mass Update Asset Master Data (57O).
Cloud Status: Deployed and started in RPADeveloper environment (confirmed in "Environments" > "Projects").
Automation Launcher: The Mass Update Asset Master Data (57O) artifact (Automation Launcher type) is configured with the main automation (MassUpdateAssetMasterData) in the "Start Manually from Agent" section.
Agent Connection to Cloud:
In the BTP lobby (Environments > RPADeveloper > Agent Management), my agent (NDTSCZ1166) appears as "Inactive" and "Connected".
In the agent's local settings (Configuration > Tenants), the connection to efdadad3trial.us10.process-automation.build.cloud.sap is "Active".

entry.title: How to display RF Message in a separate Screen - RF Screens with Touch Enablement
entry.content:
In EWM standard RF transactions, the following standard function codes are only available as function key but not as pushbutton prior to S4 HANA 2020

  • Clear input field value ('CLEAR') - F6
  • Return to previous step ('BACK') - F7
  • Display value list of actual field ('LIST') - F8
  • Display message on separate screen ('FULLMS') - F9

Example: If an error message is triggered during execution of a particular RF transaction( I.e Receiving HU by HU MO), the message is partially displayed in current session and to display full message, it is required to press 'F9' to display the full message and then press 'F7' to return to the previous Screen.

On devices without hardware function keys, these function codes either must be triggered via a software keypad or by entering the corresponding value in the shortcut field on the RF device. Both possibilities are not user-friendly and decrease the performance of the RF use

Solution: From S4 HANA 2020 onwards, SAP has released a note '2957972 - RF Framework: Add Standard Function Codes as Pushbuttons which provides to add F6, F7, F8 and F9 as an easy Push button in RF framework and easy navigation of message in a separate screen w/o usage of pressing these in RF Device.

In /SCWM/PRDVC, presentation profile, SAP has given 4 different attribute to show 'Functions as Push Button to be used based on User UI acceptability.

To use this functionality, customer have to adjust their 'Display Profile' in customizing. To display the function( F6 to F9) as push button along with 'Message display on a Pop-up message Screen' customer has to configure display profile in customizing in RF.

Extended Warehouse Management-->Mobile Data Entry-->Radio Frequency (RF) Framework-->RF Screen Manager. ( Avoid changing in standard **( 8*40) used for mounted screen on forklift  & *2 Portrait Mode( 15*26) generally used for Handheld devices.

Rather copy ** or *2 in your project requirement.

Change here Message display from '1' to '0'. Standard has be default as 1

Ensure to check the Screen manager has correct 'display message'

Good to test once above settings are performed.

Check /SCWM/RFUI in Fiori as well as in ITS( In this example, I am not using MoBgui). The required standard function codes are only available as an easy Push Button:)

Lets test any RF transaction to check 'Display Message' in a separate screen along with Std Functions as push button. 

System has added 4 std pushbuttons along with Screen specific RF pushbuttons. 

Also an easy List view is available to select the required work center( in RF REHU by MO transaction)

Displaying message in separate Screen and easy return to Previous screen.

Below example, error message are directly navigated to a separate screen( without touching F9 button) and one Press enter button navigates to original screen without pressing F7 on Handheld.

There is also a BAdI also /SCWM/EX_RF_ADD_FC_AS_PB  which allows to implement a customer own logic how the standard function codes are added to the pushbutton groups. The BAdI also has an example implementation which shows how function codes GTEXCP (to display valid exception codes), BACK and CLEAR are added to the first pushbutton group.

Thanks for reading. All tests have been performed in S4 HANA 2023 FPS02 Sandbox system.

Refences: 

2957972 - RF Framework: Add Standard Function Codes as Pushbuttons

3234853 - Error Message /SCWM/UI_RF301 while adding Buttons in tcode /SCWM/PRDVC

R&D had another look at what you are doing and suggested using this rather that 100%:

If the BestFitPage was set to False, then you had to specify the detailed Width/Height value like below: BestFitPage="false" Height="1200px" ToolPanelView="None" Width="1600px"

entry.title: Re: Custom MDF Analysis Dimension problem - Neuroinclusion
entry.content:
Hi @DariusSoreata - I don't understand why you need to enable External Code from Cust Neuroinclusion object.  This is setup as Primary Person ID so I assume the External Code contain User ID of employee.  But you only enable this column from this object.  For what? 

In Cust Neuroinclusiondetails object, you have External Code setup as Secondary Person ID, that means this field contain Person ID value of employee.  If you already have Secondary Person ID from Cust Neuroinclusiondetails object, why do you need External Code from Cust Neuroinclusion object?  The link between Primary and Secondary Person ID is already available in Employment Info table.  

Maybe try to remove Cust Neuroinclusion object entirely and see if the initial load will process successfully or not.

entry.title: Re: My First RAP Project. Many errors in Child and Parent projection view.
entry.content:
Hi Mio,

Below is my Base entity code.

************Create Interface View****************
 @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
 @EndUserText.label: 'Booking BO view'
 define view entity ZI_BOOKING_g
   as select from zbooking_data as Booking

   association [1..1] to ztravel_data        as _Travel     on  $projection.TravelUUID = _Travel.travel_uuid
   
   association [1..1] to /DMO/I_Customer           as _Customer   on  $projection.CustomerID   = _Customer.CustomerID
   association [1..1] to /DMO/I_Carrier            as _Carrier    on  $projection.CarrierID    = _Carrier.AirlineID
   association [1..1] to /DMO/I_Connection         as _Connection on  $projection.CarrierID    = _Connection.AirlineID
                                                                  and $projection.ConnectionID = _Connection.ConnectionID
   association [1..1] to /DMO/I_Flight             as _Flight     on  $projection.CarrierID    = _Flight.AirlineID
                                                                  and $projection.ConnectionID = _Flight.ConnectionID
                                                                  and $projection.FlightDate   = _Flight.FlightDate
   association [0..1] to I_Currency                as _Currency   on $projection.CurrencyCode    = _Currency.Currency    
 {
   key booking_uuid          as BookingUUID,
       travel_uuid           as TravelUUID,
       booking_id            as BookingID,
       booking_date          as BookingDate,
       customer_id           as CustomerID,
       carrier_id            as CarrierID,
       connection_id         as ConnectionID,
       flight_date           as FlightDate,
       @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
       flight_price          as FlightPrice,
       currency_code         as CurrencyCode,
       @Semantics.user.createdBy: true
       created_by            as CreatedBy,
       @Semantics.user.lastChangedBy: true
       last_changed_by       as LastChangedBy,
       @Semantics.systemDateTime.localInstanceLastChangedAt: true
       local_last_changed_at as LocalLastChangedAt,

       /* associations */
       _Travel,
       _Customer,
       _Carrier,
       _Connection,
       _Flight,
       _Currency
 }

*********************************************
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
 @EndUserText.label: 'Travel BO view'
 define view entity ZI_TRAVEL_G
   as select from ztravel_data as Travel

   association [0..*] to zbooking_data as _Booking on $projection.TravelUUID = _Booking.Travel_UUID
   
   association [0..1] to /DMO/I_Agency       as _Agency   on $projection.AgencyID = _Agency.AgencyID
   association [0..1] to /DMO/I_Customer     as _Customer on $projection.CustomerID = _Customer.CustomerID
   association [0..1] to I_Currency          as _Currency on $projection.CurrencyCode = _Currency.Currency  
 {
   key travel_uuid           as TravelUUID,
       travel_id             as TravelID,
       agency_id             as AgencyID,
       customer_id           as CustomerID,
       begin_date            as BeginDate,
       end_date              as EndDate,
       @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
       booking_fee           as BookingFee,
       @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
       total_price           as TotalPrice,
       currency_code         as CurrencyCode,
       description           as Description,
       overall_status        as TravelStatus,
       @Semantics.user.createdBy: true
       created_by            as CreatedBy,
       @Semantics.systemDateTime.createdAt: true
       created_at            as CreatedAt,
       @Semantics.user.lastChangedBy: true
       last_changed_by       as LastChangedBy,
       @Semantics.systemDateTime.lastChangedAt: true
       last_changed_at       as LastChangedAt,
       @Semantics.systemDateTime.localInstanceLastChangedAt: true
       local_last_changed_at as LocalLastChangedAt,

       /* associations */
       _Booking,
       _Agency,
       _Customer,
       _Currency      
 }

Below is my Projection entity code

 @EndUserText.label: 'Travel BO projection view'
 @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
 @search.searchable: true
 @Metadata.allowExtensions: true

 define root view entity ZC_TRAVEL_G
   as projection on ZI_TRAVEL_G as Travel
 {
   key TravelUUID,
       @search.defaultSearchElement: true
       TravelID,
       @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{ entity: { name: '/DMO/I_Agency', element: 'AgencyID'} }]
       @ObjectModel.text.element: ['AgencyName']
       @search.defaultSearchElement: true
       AgencyID,
       _Agency.Name       as AgencyName,
       @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{ entity: { name: '/DMO/I_Customer', element: 'CustomerID'} }]
       @ObjectModel.text.element: ['CustomerName']
       @search.defaultSearchElement: true
       CustomerID,
       _Customer.LastName as CustomerName,
       BeginDate,
       EndDate,
       @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
       BookingFee,
       @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
       TotalPrice,
       @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{ entity: { name: 'I_Currency', element: 'Currency'} }]
       CurrencyCode,
       Description,
       TravelStatus,
       LastChangedAt,
       LocalLastChangedAt,

       /* Associations */
       _Agency,
       _Booking : redirected to composition child ZC_BOOKING_G,
       _Currency,
       _Customer   
 }

**********************Child projection Entity***********
@EndUserText.label: 'Booking BO projection view'
 @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
 @search.searchable: true
 @Metadata.allowExtensions: true

 define view entity ZC_BOOKING_G
   as projection on ZI_BOOKING_G as Booking
 {
   key BookingUUID,
       TravelUUID,
       @search.defaultSearchElement: true
       BookingID,
       BookingDate,
       @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{ entity : {name: '/DMO/I_Customer', element: 'CustomerID'  } }]
       @ObjectModel.text.element: ['CustomerName']
       @search.defaultSearchElement: true
       CustomerID,
       _Customer.LastName as CustomerName,
       @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{entity: {name: '/DMO/I_Carrier', element: 'AirlineID' }}]
       @ObjectModel.text.element: ['CarrierName']
       CarrierID,
       _Carrier.Name      as CarrierName,
       @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [ {entity: {name: '/DMO/I_Flight', element: 'ConnectionID'},
                                            additionalBinding: [ { localElement: 'CarrierID',    element: 'AirlineID' },
                                                                 { localElement: 'FlightDate',   element: 'FlightDate',   usage: #RESULT},
                                                                 { localElement: 'FlightPrice',  element: 'Price',        usage: #RESULT },
                                                                 { localElement: 'CurrencyCode', element: 'CurrencyCode', usage: #RESULT } ] } ]
       ConnectionID,
       FlightDate,
       @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
       FlightPrice,
       @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{entity: {name: 'I_Currency', element: 'Currency' }}]
       CurrencyCode,
       LocalLastChangedAt,

       /* associations */
       _Travel : redirected to parent ZC_TRAVEL_G,
       _Customer,
       _Carrier,
       _Connection,
       _Flight
 }

This is a code snippet from SAP learning training.

 

Thank you,

Gargi

entry.title: Re: SAP Sustainability Control Tower-Import Greenhouse Gas Emissions from SAP EHS Environment Manage
entry.content:
Hi Tams,

Data collection is to define your Input method, data collection schedule for data import. Data calculation is to calculate your CO2e based on the formula that is being followed.

You have to define the collection items individually and calculate the CO2e as per each material, and can calculate category of emissions, i.e., Scope-1, Scope-2 , etc., under data calculation, and finally can have aggregation at location level.

 

Regards,

Pardha Reddy. C

entry.title: How to add a max aggregation calculation for previous year in a SAP Analytics Cloud Story?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

In SAP Analytics Cloud at the story level, how can you create a variable that captures the maximum monthly value of a numeric field for the year preceding the one selected in a page filter (i.e., that captures the highest monthly value from the prior year)?

I've already created the variable to calculate the current year's maximum monthly value, but I've been unable to build the equivalent for the prior year. The requirement is to display both the current year's and the previous year's maximum monthly values simultaneously.

The story has:

  • Input control for years (single selection) based on budat
  • Input control for months (multiple selection) based on budat
  • Analytic import model consuming from s4 onpremise via cds view

I've tried the following alternatives without success:

Alternative 1: Restricted Measure for the Prior Year based on the MAX Aggregation of the current year:

1) Create a restricted measure for the prior year based on the current year's MAX aggregation: Defined a variable to capture the maximum value of the numeric field (acdoca.hsl) using Aggregation → MAX. This didn't return the correct value for the current year—likely due to the dataset's daily granularity.

2) Workaround: Create a alculated dimension for months. Added a dimension called vMonths to represent the month of each record.

This returns all months across every year in the dataset:

3) Recreate the “current year max” variable using vMonths: Defined a new variable to get the MAX of acdoca.hsl, scoped to the vMonths dimension. This returns the expected result when the page filter selects a single year, so it correctly serves as the “current year max” value.

4) Attempt to derive the prior‑year max from the current‑year variable: Created a restricted measure atop the “current year max” variable, again scoped to vMonths and set to Select by Member → All Members. This, however, still returns the current year's maximum rather than the prior year's.

5) Test using the budat date field: Tried setting budat values via various NAVIGATION options—but all failed.

6) Test using the budat field via SELECTION: Although the variable runs, it never returns the correct prior‑year max value.

Alternative 2: Calculated Measure Using the MAX() Function.

 1) This correctly returns the sum of all monthly values from the preceding year.

2) Apply the MAX() function in a calculated measure: Use a calculated measure with the MAX() function. Instead of yielding the highest single‐month value, it still returns the full annual total for hsl.

Alternative 3: “Overall HSL MAX”

1) Define the overall‑max aggregation variable: Create a variable (hsl_max) that captures the MAX of acdoca.hsl, scoped by the budat date field.

2) Attempt to derive the prior‑year max via a restricted measure: Apply a restricted‑measure filter on hsl_max for the year before the selected one. This approach fails to return the previous year's maximum; it still yields the current selection's max value.

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated. Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Credit checks available in SAP Credit Management (FSCM)
entry.content:
Hello @aditya_2547 ,
I do not have experience with creating per segment parameters for a custom check step.
You could try to enhance view V_UKM_CHCK_STEP or V_UKM_CHCK_CRSGM. See SAP Note 1402424 - Adding own fields to V_UKM_CHCK_STEP
Or you could implement the parameters directly in the code of the customer specific check. In IO_ACCOUNT structure the credit segment is known while executing credit check coding (UKM_CHECK_STEP), so you could control with code how the check on different segments should behave.
Regards,
Edina

entry.title: Re: How to add a max aggregation calculation for previous...
entry.content:
What I tried:

  1. Create a model level calculated measure to read value from previous year: RESTRICT([GC_Fly] ,[d/Date]=Previous("Month" ,12 ) )
  2. Create a story calculated measure with aggregation MAX based on the model calculated measure of step 1. 

This gives me  the right Previous year Max value  on current year.

entry.title: Strategy Group 55: PIRs Consumption with reference to Sales Order Creation.
entry.content:
I'm working with Planned Independent Requirements (PIRs) linked to Characteristic/Configuration Data for Product Variants, and I need your insights to resolve a behavior I’ve encountered.

🎯 Objective:

Create PIRs for a product variant (Strategy Group 55) with assigned characteristics/configuration.

PIRs should only be consumed when a sales order with matching configuration is created.

PIRs should generate planned orders, but not convert to production orders until a matching sales order is received.

Lower-level components can be procured based on PIRs.

🧪 Test Scenario:

MRP View:

Configurable Material (Strategy 25): Individual/Collective = Individual

Product Variant (Strategy 55):

Individual/Collective = Collective

Assigned Configurable Material and completed characteristic assignment.

Maintained Planning Variant with characteristic values.

MD61: Created PIR for Product Variant.

MD04: PIR’s configuration was carried into the planned order, and lower-level components were planned correctly after MRP run.

⚠️ Issue / GAP Identified:
When I create a sales order with a different characteristic combination than the one in the PIR, the PIR is still being consumed - even though the configuration does not match. (other word, to be accurate 95% matches)

📌 Additional Info:

No Planning Table or Planning Profile used (per Strategy 55 behavior).

Assumption: PIR consumption is happening for closest match if no exact match is available.

Has anyone encountered this? Am I missing a key configuration or step to ensure that PIRs are only consumed for 100 % matching configurations?


Would appreciate any guidance or shared experiences!
Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Automate the Interim Account Update rule using Integration Center Job (Time Off)
entry.content:
@PetraRana 

  1. I've introduced the custom field under time account object to have the filter in the business rules.
  2. The integration center job will map the default values as "Yes"
  3. The business rule have the condition where the changes are allowed only if the integration center field is set as "Yes"
  4. On Then condition, I'm again resetting the field values as "No"

The above steps prevent the rule from executing during unrelated updates—such as imports, manual changes via Manage Data, CPI, or other integrations—appropriate conditions or safeguards should be implemented.

entry.title: G/L account renumbering for secondary cost elements
entry.content:
Hello Cloud experts,

I would like to ask your help regarding the G/L account renumbering specifications on CE 2502.

SAP help "Renumbering G/L accounts: Before You Start" describes;

  • G/L accounts not needed must be marked as Not Used during mapping.
    Check all delivered 99999* (secondary cost element) accounts before marking them as Not Used.

  • If you modify the G/L accounts for secondary cost elements, your allocation cycles will not work correctly.

Question;

  1. Is it technically possible to mark "Not used"  to Secondary cost elements ?
  2. What does it means by "MODIFY" the G/L account for secondary cost elements ?
    Renumbering is not permitted? All customers have to use the delivered "9***" secondary cost elements ?

Thank you for your support & Kindly Regards,
Ai

entry.title: Sales & Profitability IFP Calculating Sales Deductions
entry.content:
Hello All,

I hope you're doing well.

I'm currently working on creating a customized Sales & Profitability module by replicating certain dimensions and measures from the standard Integrated Financial Planning (IFP) content into a dummy planning model for demo purposes.

While calculating Net Sales using the formula:
Net Sales = Gross Revenue – Sales Deductions,
I'm facing a challenge specific to the textile industry scenario I'm planning for.

The issue lies in the commission component of Sales Deductions, which varies by customer. For example, Customer A may have a 5% commission rate, while Customer B may have 6%. This variability impacts the net sales calculation.

Could anyone please guide me on how to incorporate customer-specific commission rates into the planning model to accurately calculate Net Sales per customer? do I need to create a separate model for that, data blending capabilities, or be able to incorporate that logic in the data action of main model instead of creating another model

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: How to eliminate Rounding of percentage in SAC Planning
entry.content:
if you are using measure dimension of integer format  then it will round out decimals value to the lowest integer .
first you need to change the measure into decimal value .

even you are not filling values in your measure and filling it on gl_accounts it will behave according to the format of meausre that 
you provided in your model i.e.(integer/decimal).

entry.title: Beyond SAP Cloud - Episode 27: Terraform Run
entry.content:
This tutorial walks through the process of using Terraform to initialize and execute a script that creates directories and sub-accounts in a trial account. We demonstrate how to use the `terraform init` command to set up the necessary Terraform provider and dependencies, followed by `terraform apply` to execute the script. After confirming the execution, the script creates the directories and sub-accounts. We show the process, step by step, and emphasizes the simplicity and speed of setting up resources with Terraform.

Takeaways

  • Terraform initialization begins with running 'terraform init' in the command line to download required providers and dependencies.
  • Once initialized, 'terraform apply' is executed to run the script and implement changes.
  • Terraform first analyzes the script before execution to determine the proposed changes and asks for user confirmation.
  • The script will create directories and sub accounts as defined, and Terraform asks for user confirmation with a 'yes' response to proceed.
  • After confirming, Terraform starts the process of creating directories and sub-accounts based on the script instructions.
  • The script execution may take a few minutes, during which directories and sub-accounts are created one by one.
  • Once the process is complete, the sub accounts and directories will be available in the trial account.
  • The user can verify the successful creation by refreshing the BTP trial account to see the new directories and sub-accounts.
  • The created directories include 'directory administration' with two sub-accounts and 'directory application' with three sub-accounts.
  • Terraform streamlines the creation of complex structures, making the process of managing directories and sub accounts faster and easier.
  • The entire process of initializing, applying, and confirming Terraform scripts to set up directories and sub-accounts can be completed within minutes.

entry.title: Re: How to use Characteristic ID and Phrase Key to find l...
entry.content:
Hello

from function point of view as mentioned): normaly the demand is solved by a user.

Step 1: by using the EHS search capability a so called "hit list" is generated

Step 2: then it need to be checked by the user: is there a material assigned to the identfied objetc and if yes. list them

The issue is the second step. Here the options in SAP standard are limited

The "BAPI" is only useful for the second step and not for the first step

BUt as the first step is done (from technical perspective) by using a "function module" to which some parameter are handed over:

You can write down a E2E process which could be done 100% by an ABAP routine

Many people "hate" the EHS data model: therefore they try to extract the data to BW or other tool for doing the same story. This is possible as well.

There is a small "but":  based on the number of data the inquiry can take longer; and as the data will change over the time: any day you might get a different (but clearly correct) result

I wish the best

C.B.

entry.title: Re: RSA/ECB/PKCS1 padding Encryption in SAP ABAP
entry.content:
Has anyone resolved the issue of RSA/ECB/PKCS1Padding encryption in SAP ABAP using a public key? The public key I have is more than 600 characters long. I need to follow the RSA/ECB/PKCS1Padding standard, and I have used the function module SSF_KRN_ENVELOPE. If anyone has any idea or solution, please reply.

The steps i need to follow in ABAP : 

1.Generate 16-digit random number.Say RANDOMNO1.

2.Encrypt RANDOMNO1 using RSA/ECB/PKCS1Padding and ICICIPublicKey.

3.Encode the output of step 2 using Base64.Say ENCR_KEY.

4.Generate another 16-digit random number.Say RANDOMNO2.

5.Concatenate RANDOMNO2 and data in JSON format.Say DATA.

DATA=RANDOMNO2+data in JSONformat

6.Perform AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding encryption on DATA using RANDOMNO1 as key and RANDOMNO2 as IV.

7.Encode the output of step6 using Base64.Say ENCR_DATA.
